subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Introduction is engaging and establishes clear purpose and focus
Research question is explicitly stated, tightly focused, and drives the structure throughout
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with minimal ambiguity
weaknesses: Methodology, while generally appropriate, omits key procedural details (e.g. sampling duration, uncertainty estimation)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands in Focus and Method comfortably meet the top-band descriptors (clear purpose, precise research question), while the third strand (methodological completeness) falls just short of full detail. Given the dominant strengths and only minor methodological omissions, a score of 6/6 appropriately rewards the overall excellence of focus and clarity.

strengths: Sophisticated application of physics models to interpret data (derivations, RÂ² analyses)
Generally accurate and consistent use of technical terminology and units
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (ÎX_c), occasional missing axis units, and incomplete sentences detract slightly from precision
Some definitions (dielectric constant, SI unit derivation) lack full contextual support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates excellent subject knowledge in their derivations and application of concepts (justifying a top-band consideration), but minor lapses in terminology consistency and occasional incomplete explanations limit this to a score of 5/6 rather than the full 6.

strengths: Well-developed, coherent argument consistently tied to the research question
Quantitative analyses (percentage-error tables, reciprocal linearizations, RÂ² metrics) underpin robust evaluation
Insightful critical evaluation of modelling assumptions and suggestion of future improvements
weaknesses: Analysis lacks formal uncertainty propagation and deeper exploration of higher-order effects
Conclusion could more fully synthesize half- and full-wave comparisons quantitatively
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Critical Thinking strands fall solidly in the 7â9 band, with strong analytical depth and coherent argumentation. While the work does not reach the very highest band for consistently pervasive critique (10â12) due to missing uncertainty propagation, its overall sophistication justifies a highâband mark of 9/12.

strengths: Report structure and formatting meticulously follow IB conventions
Figures, tables, equations, and page numbering are consistently and effectively presented
weaknesses: Minor spacing quirks and occasional pixelation in graphs, but these do not impede understanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both Presentation strands meet the highest-band descriptors (clear, appropriate structure and effective layout), meriting the maximum 4/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical vocabulary (e.g., capacitance, ripple factor, rectification, impedance) is generally accurate and employed consistently in text, tables and figures.  Symbols are introduced with units and contextual definitions, and algebraic manipulation is mostly sound.  Minor lapsesâsuch as inconsistent use of ÎX_c notation, occasional missing axis units and an incomplete sentenceâshow that terminology and formatting are not flawless, aligning with the "mostly accurate" expectations of the 3-4 band.

The student builds a coherent argument linking derived inverse relationships to observed data, reinforcing claims with graphs, linear transformations and percentage-error discussions.  The comparison between half- and full-wave rectification adds breadth, and the narrative consistently circles back to the research question.  Some sectionsâparticularly the conclusionâare concise and could integrate the two rectifier cases more fully, and the rationale for dismissing anomalies lacks quantitative backing.  These limitations reduce the sophistication slightly, but the argument remains well-developed and logically structured, aligning with a solid 7 within the 7-9 band.

The student details circuit configurations, controlled variables, and justification of using the Falstad simulator, indicating generally appropriate methodological choices.  The procedure for varying capacitance and recording maxima/minima is clear and replicable, and the inclusion of both half- and full-wave rectifiers broadens the scope.  However, important operational parameters (e.g., oscilloscope time-base, sampling duration, number of cycles captured, or criteria for steady-state) are omitted; no systematic strategy for estimating random or systematic uncertainties is provided; and the exclusive reliance on a single simulation platform limits methodological robustness.  These gaps place the work solidly in the 3-4 band where methodology is "mostly complete" but not fully comprehensive.

Extensive derivations from first principles (capacitor discharge, saw-tooth RMS, ripple factor) demonstrate a sophisticated and coherent command of physics.  The student applies these models directly to interpret simulation data, employs logarithmic and Maclaurin expansions appropriately, and links circuit behaviour to concepts such as time constant, impedance and ripple factor.  Comparative tables, percentage-error calculations and RÂ² analyses further attest to a deep, accurate application of subject knowledge across both half- and full-wave contexts.

The essay includes a thoughtful reflection on modelling assumptions (saw-tooth approximation, Ï â« T, neglect of diode drop), identifies simulation limitations and evaluates percentage errors across capacitance values.  The student recognises the need for uncertainty analysis and experimental validation, suggesting future improvements.  However, evaluation remains partly qualitative; no quantitative error propagation, sensitivity analysis or alternative methods are explored in depth.  Consequently, the work demonstrates "good evaluation with some critical analysis" rather than the pervasive, nuanced critique expected for the 10-12 band.

The student introduces the topic through an engaging narrative that links personal curiosity with the broader significance of voltage smoothing, immediately signalling scope and purpose. Essential background (capacitor function, ripple voltage, rectification) is succinctly defined, and the logic of the investigationâderivation â simulation â analysis â evaluationâis spelled out in the introduction and reiterated by clear section headings. Graphics, tables and equations are consistently integrated into the prose, ensuring that a non-specialist reader can follow the argument without ambiguity. Minor stylistic lapses (occasional long sentences, duplicated figure captions) do not materially impede communication, so the work meets the descriptor of being "communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus."

Visual elements (tables, graphs, circuit diagrams) are generally clear, appropriately placed and enhance comprehension.  Axis labels, units and captions are mostly present, and colour coding supports comparison of data sets.  Nevertheless, a few graphs display pixelation, some axes lack units, and duplicate or verbose captions occasionally distract.  These minor flaws indicate an otherwise effective but not flawless layout, warranting a lower score within the 3-4 band.

The research questionâ"How does the total capacitance of the circuit affect the ripple voltage?"âis explicitly stated on the title page and re-emphasised in the introduction, hypothesis, method, analysis and conclusion.  It is narrow, quantitative and directly shapes the essayâs structure (dual investigation of half- and full-wave rectifiers, systematic variation of capacitance, matching analytical derivations).  Each section clearly aligns its content with answering the question, demonstrating sustained focus.  The formulation is neither vague nor overly broad; it permits measurable data collection and analytical treatment, fulfilling the highest-band requirement for clarity and focus.

The analysis moves logically from theoretical prediction to processed data, employing percentage-error tables, reciprocal linearisations and goodness-of-fit (RÂ²) metrics to interrogate trends.  Both half- and full-wave cases are compared quantitatively, and anomalies (e.g., 80 ÂµF bug) are identified and discussed.  Nonetheless, the absence of propagated uncertainties and a reliance on single-run simulation data constrain depth, preventing the level of âconsistently excellentâ analysis required for the top band.  Overall, the work fulfils the descriptors for a strong, clearly relevant analysis typical of the 7-9 range, meriting a mid-band score.

The report follows IB EE structural conventions meticulously: title page, contents, numbered sections, clear sub-headings, bibliography and appendices.  Equations are formatted consistently, tables and figures are sequentially labelled, and word count/page numbers are provided.  Minor issuesâsuch as an incomplete sentence in the conclusion and occasional spacing quirksâdo not undermine overall clarity, meeting the expectations of the highest presentation band (3-4).

annotations.comment: Title clearly states the investigation focus, meeting the requirement for a concise and informative title at the start of the report.
SI unit derivation for capacitance is correct, but citing a specific source or derivation steps would reinforce the validity of the units discussion.
Key terms table is effective for clarity, but some entries lack consistent unit notation; standardizing notation will improve precision.
Introduction effectively links personal curiosity to the broader context of voltage smoothing, engaging the reader and establishing purpose.
Worked calculation examples are helpful; maintain consistent spacing and unit notation throughout to avoid reader confusion.
Research question is clearly and prominently stated, providing strong focus and guiding the structure of the investigation.
Table of contents is well-structured and comprehensive, facilitating easy navigation through the reportâs sections.
Definition of ripple factor $$\rho$$ is clear; linking it explicitly to measurable circuit behavior would deepen the analysis.
Hypothesis logically follows from theoretical derivations, demonstrating strong alignment between research question and proposed outcome.
Definition of dielectric constants is accurate but could be strengthened by including the explicit equation for dielectric constant to reinforce the concept.
Explanation of impedance in RC circuits is clear and accurate, demonstrating solid understanding of AC circuit concepts.
Assumption that the post-filter waveform is sawtooth is clearly stated, underpinning the mathematical simplifications that follow.
Integral derivation of $$V_{rrms}$$ is sound, but intermediate algebraic steps are omitted; including these would improve reader comprehension.
Rationale for selecting Falstad simulator would be strengthened by describing its validation against experimental measurements or literature benchmarks.
Procedural steps are concise but omit details on how many cycles or time window were recorded for steady-state measurements.
Critical evaluation of alternative simulation tools shows strong critical thinking regarding method selection and its implications.
Raw data table includes stated uncertainties but fails to propagate them through to the processed results, limiting assessment of data reliability.
Derivation of $$-\frac{dQ}{dt}=I$$ from first principles is accurate and demonstrates a strong grasp of fundamental relationships.
Use of $$V_{min}=V_{max}e^{-T/\tau}$$ correctly links decay to ripple but would benefit from explicit discussion of the domain assumption in context.
Maclaurin approximation is applied appropriately, but evaluating the magnitude of neglected higher-order terms would strengthen the mathematical rigor.
Evaluation section synthesizes findings well; incorporating quantitative performance metrics (e.g. average % error) would clarify overall reliability.
Bibliography is comprehensive but reference formatting is inconsistent; adopting a single citation style throughout would improve presentation.
Control of diode type is noted, but consider also controlling the waveform shape (sinusoidal parameters) to eliminate additional variables.
Circuit schematic is clear; adding standardized component labels (e.g. R1, C1, D1) would improve reproducibility in the method.
Processed data effectively compares theoretical and experimental values; including uncertainty propagation in the % error columns would enhance depth of analysis.
Overlay graph of theoretical and experimental ripple voltage is informative; include trendline equation and reported RÂ² value directly in the figure caption.
Discussion on systematic bias from diode drop is insightful; quantitatively correcting for diode forward voltage would reduce low-capacitance discrepancies.
Ripple factor graph effectively displays trends; axis labels should specify that the factor is dimensionless to avoid ambiguity.
Reporting the numerical value of the RÂ² statistic in the text would substantiate claims of strong linear correlation more convincingly.
Reciprocal plot of full-wave ripple voltage is clear; discussing how its slope relates to circuit parameters would deepen critical insight.
